Switch to Dockerfile — bypass nixpacks Caddy auto-detection

nixpacks kept overriding nixpacks.toml and forcing Caddy as the
static server regardless. Dockerfile gives direct control:
node:20-alpine, npm ci, vite build, then npm start (vite preview
on $PORT).

feat: add event discovery pipeline enhancements and place-gate matcher

- Implement blog-event-discovery collector with Naver Blog Search and Gemini extraction
- Add event-cleanup to remove expired events (end_date < today-7)
- Enhance all collectors with startDate year filtering (< currentYear skip)
- Implement place-gate matcher for centralized place insert validation
- Add kakao-enrich for event lat/lng backfill and kakaoLimiter import fix
- Add collection-log and prefetch utility modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rewrite screenshot to use KWin ScreenShot2, strip portal to input-only

- portal_helper.py: Remove all GStreamer/frame capture code (Gst, GstApp,
  _grab_frame, PipeWire fd management). Helper now only handles session
  creation and input (pointer_move, pointer_click, key_press, close).

- portal.py: Remove grab_frame() method from PortalClient.

- tools/desktop.py: screenshot() now uses kwin_screenshot module
  (capture_active_window, capture_screen) with coordinate metadata.
  Returns [Image, metadata_text] with origin/scale info. Default target
  changed from "desktop" to "window". New "monitor" target added.
  Module-level _last_screenshot_meta tracks coordinates for send_input
  offset mapping. _send_mouse applies origin+scale offset when portal
  is connected and metadata is available.

- tests/test_tools_desktop.py: Rewritten to test new screenshot behavior
  (capture_active_window mock), coordinate offset in send_input, and
  monitor/desktop targets.

commit.message:
fix: traffic_limit now compares total allocated data, not consumed traffic

Previously remaining_traffic used users_usage (consumed bytes = 0 on fresh
installs), so any user with data_limit < traffic_limit could always be created,
making the budget check useless.

Now checks against the SUM of all existing users' data_limits under the admin,
so 8GB + 4GB = 12GB correctly exceeds a 10GB traffic_limit.

Also fixes modify_user to exclude the current user from the sum.

Additionally: merge 15 divergent Alembic migration heads into one so that
'alembic upgrade head' no longer fails; change Dockerfile from ';' to '&&'
so the app won't silently start with an unapplied schema.

commit.message:
feat(graph): replace D3 with Cytoscape.js interactive skill map

Upgrades the skill graph from a basic D3 force simulation to a
full-featured Cytoscape.js knowledge map.

Node types and colors:
- Green  = skills you already have
- Blue   = next learnable skills (prerequisites met)
- Grey   = locked skills (missing prerequisites)
- Purple = shortest roadmap path to target role

Node sizing by importance:
- Large (58px) = core foundations (Python, Linear Algebra, Statistics)
- Medium (42px) = important domain skills (PyTorch, Docker, React)
- Small  (30px) = tools and minor skills

Domain accent border rings per domain (Programming/Math/ML/Engineering/Web/Data)

Interaction features:
- Click node: selects, dims outside neighbourhood, shows tooltip with
  prerequisites + dependents
- Hover glow effect
- Drag, scroll-to-zoom, pan (Cytoscape built-in)

Roadmap path highlighting:
- Path nodes in purple with thicker border
- Path edges in purple and thicker
- Animated pulse on load (expand/flash)

Filter controls panel above graph:
- Full Graph / Roadmap Path / Missing Skills / Fit View

Layout: cose (Compound Spring Embedder) — no overlaps, readable clusters

Animations: node fade-in after layout; roadmap path pulse 650ms later

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bake scope appID var into desktop app builds (#582)

* Bake scope appID var into desktop app builds

Signed-off-by: Max Holland <max@livepeer.org>

* Use explicit undefined check for runtime env override

Check `process.env.SCOPE_CLOUD_APP_ID === undefined` instead of
truthiness so that setting the env var to an empty string at runtime
still takes precedence over the baked-in build-time value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Rafał Leszko <rafal@livepeer.org>

commit.message:
fix(ui): clean up window event listeners and ResizeObserver in destroy() (#831)

Multiple components added window-level event listeners using anonymous
callbacks, making them impossible to remove later. This caused memory
leaks as listeners accumulated across component lifecycles.

- CountryTimeline: store theme-changed handler, remove in destroy()
- DeckGLMap: store theme-changed handler, remove in destroy()
- Map: store theme-changed handler, promote ResizeObserver from local
  variable to class property, disconnect and remove both in destroy()

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove redundant Orders page - use native Shopify Orders instead

Configuration details (color, material, price) are already visible in
Shopify's native Orders page as line item properties. Having a separate
Orders page in the app was redundant and confusing UX.

- Remove Orders link from navigation
- Delete app.configurations.tsx and app.configurations.$id.tsx
- Delete webhooks.orders.create.tsx
- Remove orders/create webhook from shopify.app.toml
- Remove read_orders scope (no longer needed)

Merchants should use Shopify's native Orders page for fulfillment.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Add error handlers to transfer and notification mutations, fix method fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add avatar upload UI and extract reusable file upload component

Extract drag-and-drop file selection into shared FileUploadArea component,
create AvatarUpload dialog for profile picture updates, and consolidate
image URL logic into a shared getImageUrl utility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement full Polymarket orderbook system (v1)

7-crate Rust workspace: pb-types (fixed-point arithmetic, zero-copy wire
types), pb-book (BTreeMap L2 orderbook), pb-feed (WebSocket/REST ingest
with reconnection and rate limiting), pb-store (Parquet + ClickHouse
sinks), pb-replay (historical book reconstruction), pb-metrics
(Prometheus counters/histograms via axum), pb-bin (CLI with discover,
ingest, replay, backfill subcommands).

Includes 44 passing tests (29 unit + 15 integration), Criterion
benchmarks, OpenSpec design docs, CI workflow, Dockerfile, and
supporting infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix GoReleaser Docker build context error

GoReleaser passes only the pre-built binary to the Docker build context,
so the regular Dockerfile (which builds from source) fails. Add a minimal
Dockerfile.goreleaser that simply copies the binary into an Alpine image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 3 bugs from deep scan: effect deps, cleanup, regex, JSONB safety

- ProductResult: use stable primitives (total_score, ingredients) in
  useEffect deps instead of product object reference to prevent
  unnecessary Spoonacular API calls on every re-render
- ProductResult: add cancellation flag to Spoonacular useEffect to
  prevent state update on unmounted component (memory leak)
- recipes.js: preserve digits/hyphens in ingredient regex so
  "Vitamin B12" and "omega-3" survive parsing for Spoonacular
- recipes.js: use Array.isArray() on replaces_products JSONB before
  checking .length (pre-existing type safety fix)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(argocd): use wildcard namespace for cluster AppProject

Enumerating namespaces for the cluster project is fragile — platform
apps deploy resources across many namespaces (cilium-secrets, tailscale,
woodpecker CNPs, etc). Use namespace wildcard for cluster project since
it needs broad access. The user project remains restricted to apps,
garage, and woodpecker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: add CLAUDE.md with dev-to-prod best practices

Encodes 5 mandatory rules to prevent recurrence of past incidents:
1. Always use migrations (never alter schema manually)
2. Never write to production DB directly
3. Cherry-pick critical fixes immediately, don't wait for full merge
4. Run pre-merge risk investigation before every dev→prod push
5. Tag prod releases after every merge to main

Initial release: VisionSharp v0.1.0

Industrial vision inspection framework for .NET 8 with 16 modules:
Core pipeline engine, 8 operators, filters, geometry, tools, calibration,
communication (Modbus TCP), camera, I/O, drawing, color, barcode, PatMax,
OCR, inference (ONNX), and web dashboard.

110 unit tests, CI/CD workflows, NuGet publishing pipeline.

commit.message:
Rewrite notification pages: 3 independent cards, consistent vocabulary

- SettingsPage: each notification type (Audit complete, Rules failed, Score
  dropped) is now its own independent SettingsCard with toggle + options +
  inline Preview email button; rules_failed and trend_down are now independently
  toggleable instead of a single radio group forcing one choice
- ProjectNotificationsPage: same 3-card structure; when useDefaults is on shows
  InheritedChip per card; when off shows live controls; adds auditFormat override;
  adds per-card preview buttons; removes opacity dimming pattern
- EmailPreviewDialog: update TITLES to match new vocabulary (Score dropped)
- HelpPage: fix 'Trend down' → 'Score dropped' in notifications section
- Vocabulary aligned everywhere: Audit complete / Rules failed / Score dropped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve P0-P1 bugs and add CHANGELOG for v2 release

P0: Fix Parquet layer rename deleting DuckDB table — store original
tableName on LayerInfo so renames don't affect table identity.

P1 fixes:
- Shapefile layers now use actual file name instead of hardcoded
  'shapefile_layer'
- Numeric filter uses Number() conversion for string-typed CSV values
  and guards against empty input
- Escape file names in read_parquet() SQL to handle special characters
- Remove dead WKT code (geometryToWKT, wktToGeometry) and unused
  imports; resolve all tsc --noEmit errors (0 remaining)
- Pin DuckDB CDN URLs to installed package version (1.33.1-dev20.0)

Also: bump version to 2.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md with v2 features and
known issues for future releases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Streamable HTTP transport for MCP server

- `velos mcp-server` — stdio transport (local, unchanged)
- `velos mcp-server --port 8080` — Streamable HTTP transport (remote)
  POST /mcp for JSON-RPC, GET /mcp for SSE, GET /health
- Enables VPS → local AI client workflow (Velos on server,
  Claude Code/Desktop/Cursor on laptop)
- Updated README with MCP setup instructions for all AI clients
- Made McpServer.handle_method and JsonRpcError public for HTTP handler

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
